checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the output JSON against the source PDF. The metadata title and image alt texts are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility. The 'omitted' field is a pipeline marker indicating missing content, not a fabrication of the content itself. All other headings and paragraphs in the output correspond to text found in the original document.

I have verified all contact information including the phone number (203-704-0581) and email (D.Jewiss@me.com) against the source. There are no instances of fabricated contact information in the output. While some contact info was omitted along with entire pages, no information present in the output is incorrect or invented.

The output document has massive content omissions. A placeholder in the JSON explicitly states that 28 sections were omitted. This includes the entire RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview (pages 7-17), the 'Saving Lives Together' brochure (pages 18-27), the Cardiac Arrest Case Review section (pages 29-33), the introductory page for the D-RAK training (page 34), and the 'Training Payment Options' page (page 36). These are authoritative sections of the source document that are completely absent from the converted output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 7-17, 18-27, 29-33, 34, and 36 of the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON contains an explicit marker: '{"_omitted": "28 sections omitted for token budget"}'. No content from these pages appears between elements 18 and 355 or between 361 and 458.
checks.b_evidence_location: Middle of the document, specifically between reading_order 18 and 355, and 361 and 458.

checks.a_analysis: The tables present in the output (Agenda on p.1, Attendance/Votes on p.3-6, Comparison on p.28, and D-RAK details on p.35) have been checked for row counts. Each table contains all the data rows from the source document. For example, the Curriculum Overview Comparison table correctly includes all 13 comparison criteria.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In Section 8 of the meeting minutes (page 4), the text is truncated. The output element ends abruptly mid-word, and the next element starts at the next paragraph, resulting in the loss of several words that complete the sentence. This constitutes a significant omission within an otherwise present section.
checks.b_evidence_source: approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current course list for the subscription at 90%
checks.b_evidence_output: ...approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current co

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the truncation noted in C5, the text that is present faithfully represents the wording of the source. Minor reformatting such as converting red text to italics for emphasis is observed and considered acceptable. However, the truncation in C5 alters the information a user would receive.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for repeated text blocks, headers, or footers that do not appear multiple times in the source. Content appears once in the output corresponding to its single appearance in the source PDF.

In the Minutes section (starting at index 14), the title is assigned as an H1. The subsequent sectional items (specifically item 6 at index 20) are assigned as H3. This results in a skipped H2 level, breaking the heading hierarchy necessary for accessibility and semantic organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: The section '6. Technology Update' follows the main title 'North Carolina 911 Board Education Committee Meeting MINUTES'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Index 14 is a level 1 heading. Index 20 is a level 3 heading.

checks.a_analysis: The tables found in the agenda, minutes, and curriculum comparison sections were analyzed. The output correctly identifies header rows and maintains the column/row relationships found in the source, including complex layouts like the comparison grid on page 28.

The flow of information in the output generally follows the source. Minor adjustments for accessibility, such as moving the logo to the end of a section or pulling sub-bullets out of a table cell into a subsequent list, are acceptable.

Two failures were identified. First, the numbered list of agenda items in the minutes (1-11) is fragmented across different element types: items 1-5 are in a list element (index 16), items 6-8 are heading elements (indices 20, 25, 26), and items 9-11 are paragraph elements (indices 30, 31, 34). This destroys the semantic coherence of the list. Second, the clearly numbered lists in the 'Goals' section are incorrectly typed as 'unordered' lists in the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list 1-11 in the Minutes section (pages 3-6) and Numbered lists 1-4 in the Goals section (page 37).
checks.b_evidence_output: Items 1-11 are split into 'list', 'heading', and 'paragraph' types (indices 16-34). Goals lists at indices 490 and 492 are marked 'list_type': 'unordered'.

checks.a_analysis: The contact information on page 35 includes an email address (D.Jewiss@me.com) and a website (www.TheRAKAcademy.com). These are missing from the output text stream, which splits the paragraph into two fragments (indices 467 and 469) exactly where the link content should reside.
checks.b_evidence_source: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581... www.TheRAKAcademy.com
checks.b_evidence_output: Index 467: 'Please contact Dan Jewiss at '. Index 469: ' or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training.'
checks.b_evidence_location: RAK Academy contact info, page 35, indices 467-469
